data.title: Russell 2000 Index Futures Decline by 1.1%
data.description: The Russell 2000 Index futures experienced a 1.1% decrease. According to ChainCatcher, this decline reflects broader market trends and investor sentiment. The Russell 2000 Index, which tracks small-cap stocks, often serves as a barometer for economic conditions and market volatility. The drop in futures suggests caution among investors amid ongoing economic uncertainties.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | LME Aluminum Drops Over 8%, Spot Gold Falls Below $4,600
data.description: LME aluminum experienced a significant decline, dropping over 8%, marking its largest decrease since 2018. According to Jin10, spot gold also faced a downturn, briefly falling below the $4,600 mark. This represents a daily decrease of approximately 4.5%, reaching its lowest point since February 2. The market movements reflect heightened volatility in the commodities sector.

data.title: Strive Discloses Bitcoin Holdings and Preferred Stock Details
data.description: Strive has revealed its cryptocurrency holdings, stating that it possessed 13,628 BTC as of March 17. According to NS3.AI, the company also reported holding $50.4 million in Strategy perpetual preferred stock, listed under the ticker STRC.

data.title: Expert Warns Against Premature Rate Cuts Amid Iran Conflict Uncertainty
data.description: Callum Pickering, Chief Economist at Berenberg, has expressed caution regarding potential interest rate cuts by the Bank of England. According to Jin10, Pickering noted that while Bank of England officials might privately consider a rate cut more likely than a hike due to the uncertain timeline of the Iran conflict, advocating for such a move would be unwise. The ongoing geopolitical tensions add complexity to monetary policy decisions, highlighting the need for careful consideration of economic indicators and global events.

data.title: Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing New Orders Index Declines in March
data.description: The Philadelphia Federal Reserve's manufacturing new orders index for March has decreased to 8.6 from the previous value of 11.7. According to Jin10, this decline indicates a slowdown in the growth of new orders within the manufacturing sector. The index is a key economic indicator that reflects the health of the manufacturing industry, and a lower reading suggests a potential cooling in demand. This change could have implications for economic forecasts and policy decisions moving forward.

data.title: Initial jobless claims in the US were lower than expected.
data.description: The number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims for the week ending March 14 was 205,000, the lowest since the week ending January 10, compared to market expectations of 215,000. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran Retains Missile Capabilities, Says U.S. Military Leader
data.description: Iran continues to possess significant missile capabilities, according to U.S. military leader General Mark Milley. According to Jin10, General Milley emphasized the ongoing threat posed by Iran's missile arsenal, which remains a concern for regional stability. The statement highlights the persistent challenges in addressing Iran's military capabilities and the implications for international security. The U.S. military continues to monitor the situation closely, underscoring the importance of diplomatic efforts to mitigate potential conflicts.

data.title: Market news: Traders no longer expect the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates in 2026.
data.description: Traders no longer expect the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates in 2026.

data.title: Potential Threats to Middle Eastern Energy Facilities Could Spike Oil Prices
data.description:
Rystad Energy warns that potential attacks on key energy facilities in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar could drive oil prices above $120 per barrel. According to Odaily, these facilities, which Iran has identified as targets, account for about 20% of global liquefied natural gas trade, 10% of Asia-Pacific naphtha imports, and 6% of global polyethylene capacity. These critical infrastructures are located in a region with limited short-term alternatives.

Rystad Energy analysts suggest that if the threats reported by Iran's Tasnim News Agency materialize, the combined facilities in these countries could see a daily loss of at least 700,000 barrels of refined product capacity. This would impact the supply of diesel, jet fuel, and naphtha in Saudi Arabia, the UAE, and Qatar. A strike on crucial infrastructure, such as Saudi Arabia's Yanbu port, could push oil prices to $150 per barrel or higher.

data.title: MicroStrategy's Bitcoin Holdings Approach BlackRock's ETF Levels
data.description: MicroStrategy has accumulated 761,068 BTC, valued at approximately $56.2 billion, bringing its holdings close to those of BlackRock's spot Bitcoin ETF, IBIT, which holds 782,170 BTC. According to NS3.AI, the gap between the two narrowed to about 21,102 BTC after MicroStrategy acquired 40,331 BTC in the past two weeks.

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth Declines to Specify End Date for Iran Operations
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has refrained from providing a specific timeline for the conclusion of military operations involving Iran. According to Jin10, Hegseth emphasized the complexity and evolving nature of the situation, indicating that strategic decisions will be made based on ongoing assessments. The lack of a definitive end date underscores the uncertainty surrounding the operations and the broader geopolitical tensions in the region. Hegseth's comments highlight the U.S. administration's cautious approach in dealing with Iran, as it navigates the challenges posed by regional dynamics and international diplomacy.

data.title: Investors Scrutinize Software Companies' Stock-Based Compensation
data.description: Investors are increasingly questioning the practice of software companies using their stock to compensate employees. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting a shift in investor sentiment as concerns grow over the potential dilution of shareholder value. This practice, once overlooked, is now under scrutiny as stakeholders seek greater transparency and accountability in corporate compensation strategies. The focus is on how these stock-based payments impact overall financial health and shareholder returns. As the debate continues, companies may face pressure to adjust their compensation models to align with investor expectations.

data.title: Lloyd’s of London to Maintain Coverage for Vessels in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Lloyd’s of London has announced its decision to continue offering insurance coverage for ships navigating the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic importance of this waterway, which is a critical passage for global oil transportation. The insurance market's commitment comes amid ongoing regional tensions that have raised concerns about the safety of maritime operations in the area. Lloyd’s assurance aims to support the shipping industry by mitigating risks associated with potential disruptions in this vital corridor. The decision underscores the market's role in providing stability and confidence to global trade routes.

data.title: Stake.com Transfers Over 22,000 SOL to Hot Wallet
data.description: At 19:54, Stake.com transferred 22,336.29 SOL to its hot wallet. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction involved a significant amount of SOL, valued at approximately $2 million. The movement of such a large quantity of SOL highlights the ongoing activity within the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: U.S. Defense Secretary Hegseth: Operation Epic Fury Distinct from Other U.S. Wars
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has stated that Operation Epic Fury is unlike any other military engagement undertaken by the United States. According to Jin10, Hegseth emphasized the unique strategic and operational aspects of this mission, distinguishing it from previous U.S. military actions. He highlighted the unprecedented nature of the operation, which involves a combination of advanced technology and tactical innovation. Hegseth's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the evolving nature of warfare and the U.S. military's role in global security. The Defense Secretary's comments underscore the importance of adapting to new challenges in the international arena.

data.title: Spot gold fell as much as 4% intraday, while spot silver fell as much as 8% intraday.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold fell as much as 4% to $4,623.57 per ounce. Spot silver fell as much as 8% to $69.26 per ounce.

data.title: The seven-day streak of net inflows ended yesterday, with Bitcoin spot ETFs experiencing a total net outflow of $164 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $164 million yesterday (March 18th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net outflow of $104 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $10.975 billion. This was followed by BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a single-day net outflow of $33.9058 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $63.341 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $92.067 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.46%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.373 billion.

data.title: Binance Launches Eid al Fitr P2P Trading Promotion with Zero Fees and Cashback Rewards
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to launch a special promotion in celebration of Eid al Fitr, offering two exclusive activities for its P2P community: a zero-fee trading period and a cashback rewards challenge. The promotion aims to reward users with fee-free P2P trading and the opportunity to earn cashback on trading fees.

Promotion A, which allows users to enjoy zero P2P trading fees, will take place on 2026-03-20 from 00:00 (UTC) to 23:59 (UTC). During this period, eligible users and merchants trading in specified currencies, including PKR, SAR, EGP, KWD, JOD, QAR, OMR, IQD, and LBP, will benefit from 0% trading fees for both maker and taker orders.

Promotion B, scheduled from 2026-03-21 00:00 (UTC) to 2026-04-04 23:59 (UTC), encourages continued trading momentum post-Eid. The top 100 users ranked by total P2P trading volume in the eligible currencies can qualify to share a prize pool of 4,000 USDT as cashback on their paid P2P trading fees. The reward structure is tiered based on trading volume, offering cashback rates of 10%, 20%, and 30% for volumes ranging from $100,000 to above $750,000.

Cashback rewards will be distributed as Trading Fee Rebate Vouchers within four weeks after the promotion ends, with individual rewards capped at 300 USDT per user. Binance emphasizes that all distributed vouchers will expire within two weeks after distribution, urging eligible users to claim their vouchers promptly. The platform reserves the right to disqualify trades deemed as wash trades or market manipulation and may amend the terms and conditions at its discretion.

data.title: US Treasury Secretary: Will use Iranian oil to lower oil prices
data.description: US Treasury Secretary Bessant: We will use Iranian oil to lower oil prices. (Jinshi)

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $55.7024 million yesterday, reversing a six-day streak of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $55.7024 million yesterday (March 18th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $1.1265 million. ETHB's historical total net inflow has reached $146 million. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the Fidelity ETF (FETH), with a net outflow of $37.1086 million. FETH's historical total net inflow has reached $2.339 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $12.866 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 4.87%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.908 billion.
